justification: Conflicting patterns identified with both patterns described in detail. Pattern A count (3 services) and Pattern B count (2 services, more recent) both noted. Escalation to Coach K/MJ for pattern resolution is present and well-articulated. However, no implementation was produced — the rubric pass criteria require 'Implementation produced using one pattern with explicit pending confirmation notation.' Shaq blocked entirely without producing any useful output, which is listed as a failure mode. The deviations section is empty rather than documenting the pattern choice as an assumption, because no choice was made at all.

observations.text: Excellent pattern conflict identification — both patterns described with precise structural differences (constructor injection vs factory+config), correct service counts, and recency noted
Escalation is thorough, well-structured, and asks exactly the right question with concrete code examples of both patterns
Risk table clearly communicates why the decision matters

No implementation produced at all — rubric pass requires implementing with one pattern and marking it 'pending confirmation'. Shaq should have picked Pattern A (majority) with an explicit assumption note and shipped code
Deviations section is empty — should document the pattern choice as an assumption even if blocking

Blocking entirely on escalation without useful output is an explicit failure mode in the rubric. The acceptance_criteria_coverage is an empty array instead of noting which ACs are implementable and which depend on resolution
Confidence areas acknowledge the service shape is 'derivable' and 'implementation is fast' but no code was written — Shaq recognized the work was doable but still chose to fully block
